title: BUG: Series.loc slicing raises KeyError unexpectedly
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [ ] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd

print(pd.Series([3, 6, 7, 6], index=[3, 6, 7, 9]).loc[5:], "\n")  # works
print(pd.Series([3, 6, 7, 6], index=[3, 6, 7, 6]).loc[5:], "\n")  # raises error
```

### Issue Description

When an index value is repeated, loc slicing raises a `KeyError`. 
The [documentation](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.Series.loc.html#pandas.Series.loc) does not mention this as a condition for raising a `KeyError`.



### Expected Behavior

I think this should return `d.Series([6, 7, 6], index=[6, 7, 6])` and not raise an error.

title: BUG: DataFrame.loc[] returns object type instead of float
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
from pandas import DataFrame


df_e = DataFrame([["x", 10.0, 20.0], ["y", 30.0, 40.0]])
print(df_e.loc[0, slice(1, 3)])
```

### Issue Description

When selecting columns using slices from a DataFrame that contains mixed data types, the resulting columns always have the dtype object, even when all the selected elements share the same data type (for example, float64). This issue was previously reported and fixed for array-based column selection in Issue #60600, but it still occurs when selecting columns with slices.


### Expected Behavior

```
# Current Output: 
1    10.0
2    20.0
Name: 0, dtype: object


# Expected Output: 
1    10.0
2    20.0
Name: 0, dtype: float64
```

title: BUG: pd.Grouper converts PyArrow timestamp to numpy datetime64
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd
import io

data = '''agency_cd,site_no,datetime,tz_cd,144166_00060,144166_00060_cd,144167_00065,144167_00065_cd
USGS,9333500,2001-05-07 01:00:00,MDT,71.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-07 01:15:00,MDT,71.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-07 01:30:00,MDT,71.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-07 01:45:00,MDT,70.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-07 02:00:00,MDT,70.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-08 02:15:00,MDT,69.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-08 02:30:00,MDT,70.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-08 02:45:00,MDT,70.0,A:[91],,
USGS,9333500,2001-05-08 03:00:00,MDT,70.0,A:[91],,'''

df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(data), dtype_backend='pyarrow', engine='pyarrow')

def to_denver_time(df_, time_col, tz_col):
    return (df_
     .assign(**{tz_col: df_[tz_col].replace('MDT', 'MST7MDT')})
     .groupby(tz_col)
     [time_col]
     .transform(lambda s: pd.to_datetime(s)
         .dt.tz_localize(s.name, ambiguous=True)
         .dt.tz_convert('America/Denver'))
     .astype('timestamp[ns, America/Denver][pyarrow]')
    )

def tweak_river(df_):
    return (df_
     .assign(datetime=to_denver_time(df_, 'datetime', 'tz_cd'))
     .rename(columns={'144166_00060': 'cfs'})
     .loc[:, ['datetime', 'cfs']]
     .set_index('datetime')
    )

dd = tweak_river(df)

# Check the dtype before grouping
print("Before pd.Grouper:")
print(f"Index dtype: {dd.index.dtype}")

# Use pd.Grouper to group by day
result = dd.groupby(pd.Grouper(freq='D')).median(numeric_only=True)

# Check the dtype after grouping
print("\nAfter pd.Grouper:")
print(f"Index dtype: {result.index.dtype}")
```

### Issue Description

When using `pd.Grouper` with a PyArrow-backed timestamp column, the resulting index is automatically converted from the PyArrow dtype (`timestamp[ns, America/Denver][pyarrow]`) to the numpy-backed dtype (`datetime64[ns, America/Denver]`). This conversion happens silently and is inconsistent with the goal of maintaining PyArrow types throughout the analysis pipeline.

This affects users working with PyArrow backend who want to maintain PyArrow types for performance and consistency reasons.

### Expected Behavior

The resulting index after using `pd.Grouper` should maintain the original PyArrow dtype (`timestamp[ns, America/Denver][pyarrow]`) instead of converting to numpy's `datetime64[ns, America/Denver]`.

### Installed Versions

title: BUG: concatenating Series with overlapping Interval names fails
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd

value_index = pd.IntervalIndex.from_tuples([(0.0, 1.0), (1.0, 2.0)], name="foo")
level_index = pd.IntervalIndex.from_tuples([(0.0, 10.0), (0.0, 20.0)], name="bar")

values = [
    pd.Series([1.0, 3.0], name=pd.Interval(0.0, 10.0), index=value_index),
    pd.Series([5.0, 7.0], name=pd.Interval(0.0, 20.0), index=value_index),
]

result = pd.concat(values, keys=level_index, levels=[level_index], names=["bar"])

expected = pd.Series(
    [1.0, 3.0, 5.0, 7.0], index=pd.MultiIndex.from_product([level_index, value_index])

pd.testing.assert_series_equal(result, expected)
```

title: BUG: `DataFrame.any`/`all(skipna=False)` raises ValueError instead of returning NA
body:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd

s = pd.Series([False, None], dtype="boolean")
df = s.to_frame("a")

s.any(skipna=False)
df.any(skipna=False)
```

### Issue Description

`DataFrame.any(skipna=False)` and `DataFrame.all(skipna=False)` raise `ValueError: cannot convert float NaN to bool` on numpy-nullable columns (`boolean`, `Int*`, `UInt*`, `Float*`) whenever the reduction would propagate `pd.NA`.

The corresponding `Series.any/all(skipna=False)` reductions on the same data correctly return `pd.NA`.

### Expected Behavior

`DataFrame.any(skipna=False)` and `DataFrame.all(skipna=False)` should return a `Series` with nullable boolean dtype (`boolean`) containing `pd.NA` when the corresponding `Series.any/all(skipna=False)` reduction on the same column returns `pd.NA`.

title: Consider bumping C standard in meson.build from C99 to C17
body:
### Describe the bug

Currently, trying to build scikit-learn with the python 3.13 free-threaded build leads to a compilation error related to usage of `static_assert` in CPython internals. This leaks into public code via cython's adding `#include "internal/pycore_frame.h"` to module init code.

See https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/20515 where scipy made a similar change for similar reasons.

C17 is well-supported by downstream compilers, including MSVC. CPython itself is built with C11, which is a superset of C17.

Opening this as an issue instead of just making a pull request to see if there are good reasons besides inertia why `meson.build` specifies C99.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```bash
python -m pip install -v . --no-build-isolation
```

### Expected Results

successful build

### Actual Results


title: TypeError when fitting GridSearchCV or RandomizedSearchCV with OrdinalEncoder and OneHotEncoder in parameters grid
body:
### Describe the bug

Having both `OrdinalEncoder` and `OneHotEncoder` inside the parameters grid to be used by the `GridSearchCV` or `RandomizedSearchCV` results in the following error: `TypeError: float() argument must be a string or a real number, not 'OneHotEncoder'`.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn import set_config
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.ensemble import HistGradientBoostingRegressor
from sklearn.model_selection import GridSearchCV, RandomizedSearchCV
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, OrdinalEncoder

set_config(transform_output="pandas")

# Setting seed for reproducibility
np.random.seed(42)

# Create a DataFrame with 1000 rows and 5 columns
num_rows = 1000
data = {
    "numeric_1": np.random.randn(num_rows),  # Normally distributed random numbers
    "numeric_3": np.random.randint(
        1, 100, size=num_rows
    ),  # Random integers between 1 and 100
    "object_1": np.random.choice(
        ["A", "B", "C", "D"], size=num_rows
    ),  # Random choice among 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D'
    "object_2": np.random.choice(
        ["X", "Y", "Z"], size=num_rows
    ),  # Random choice among 'X', 'Y', 'Z'
    "target": np.random.rand(num_rows)
    * 100,  # Uniformly distributed random numbers [0, 100)
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

X = df.drop("target", axis=1)
y = df["target"]

enc = ColumnTransformer(
    [("enc", OneHotEncoder(sparse_output=False), ["object_1", "object_2"])],
    remainder="passthrough",
    verbose_feature_names_out=False,
)

pipe = Pipeline(
    [
        ("enc", enc),
        ("regressor", HistGradientBoostingRegressor()),
    ]
)

grid_params = {
    "enc__enc": [
        OneHotEncoder(sparse_output=False),
        OrdinalEncoder(),
    ]
}

grid_search = GridSearchCV(pipe, grid_params, cv=5)
grid_search.fit(X, y)
# RandomizedSearchCV produces the same error
```

title: GaussianNB(priors=...) is useless
body:
### Describe the bug

If I set the class priors to be very small for classes 0 and 2 and very large for class 1, I expect my predictions to be of class 1. However, I get class 0. It seems to be that `GaussianNB(priors=...)` is useless.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
from sklearn import datasets
from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB

# Load data
iris = datasets.load_iris()
features = iris.data
target = iris.target

# Create Gaussian naive Bayes object
classifer = GaussianNB()  # The prior is adjusted based on the data
# Train model
model = classifer.fit(features, target)
# Create new observation
new_observation = [[5.2, 3.6, 1.5, 0.3]]
# Predict class
model.predict(new_observation)
# array([0])

# Set prior probabilities p(y) of each class of 3 (does not work)
clf = GaussianNB(priors=[1e-12, 1-1e-11, 9e-12])
# Train model
model_priors = clf.fit(features, target)
# Create new observation
new_observation = [[5.2, 3.6, 1.5, 0.3]]
# Predict class
model_priors.predict(new_observation)
# array([0])
```

### Expected Results

- `array([0])` for `GaussianNB` without priors
- `array([1])` for `GaussianNB(priors=...)` with priors

### Actual Results

- `array([0])` for `GaussianNB` without priors
- `array([0])` for `GaussianNB(priors=...)` with priors

title: Gaussian Mixture: Diagonal covariance vectors might contain unreasonably negative values when the input datatype is np.float32
body:
### Describe the bug

The Gaussian Mixture implementation shows numerical instabilities on single-precision floating point input numbers, that even large values of the regularization parameter reg_covar (like 0.1) cannot mitigate.

More specifically, diagonal covariance elements must not be negative. However, due to the numerical instabilities intrinsic to floating point arithmetic, they might end up being tiny negative numbers that reg_covar must compensate.
It turns out that, for some input float32 , the covariance can reach the unreasonable value of -0.99999979.
This is because squaring float32 numbers significantly magnifies their precision errors.

The proposed solution consists in converting float32 values to float64 before squaring them.
Care must be taken to not increase memory consumption in the overall process.
Hence, as avgX_means is equal to avg_means2, the return value can be simplified.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
import numpy as np
from sklearn.mixture import GaussianMixture

model = GaussianMixture(n_components=2, covariance_type="spherical", reg_covar=0.1)
model.fit(np.array([[9999.0], [0.0]], dtype=np.float32))
model.covariances_
```

### Expected Results

```python
array([0.1, 0.1])
```

title: Metadata not routed to transformers in pipeline during cross validation.
body:
### Describe the bug

When using a pipeline with transformers in combination with cross validation, it seems that metadata is not correctly routed to the transformers during prediction. I would expect, that if `set_transform_request` is set, that this is honored when calling predict on the pipeline.

**Edit:** At least according to the code this is a known limitation. Although I couldn't find an issue tracking the progress on this.
https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/9028b518e7a906a806a1dc8994f2714cc980c941/sklearn/model_selection/_validation.py#L362C1-L367C14

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
import sklearn
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.datasets import make_regression
from sklearn.base import TransformerMixin, _MetadataRequester
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_validate

print(sklearn.__version__)

sklearn.set_config(enable_metadata_routing=True)


class DummyTransfomerWithMetadata(TransformerMixin, _MetadataRequester):

    def fit(self, X, y=None, metadata=None):
        return self

    def transform(self, X, y=None, metadata=None):
        print(f"Received {metadata=}")
        return X

    # We need to explicitly implement fit_transform,
    # otherwise transform will not receive metadata during fit
    def fit_transform(self, X, y=None, metadata=None):
        return self.transform(X, y, metadata)


X, y = make_regression()

transformer = DummyTransfomerWithMetadata()
transformer.set_fit_request(metadata=True)
transformer.set_transform_request(metadata=True)


pipe = Pipeline([
    ("transformer", transformer),
    ("clf", LinearRegression())
])


print(f"--- Cross validation ---")
cross_validate(
    pipe, X, y, params={"metadata": "Some metadata"}, cv=2
)
```

### Expected Results

```
1.7.0
--- Cross validation ---
Received metadata='Some metadata'   # Fit 
Received metadata='Some metadata'   # Predict
Received metadata='Some metadata'
Received metadata='Some metadata'
```

### Actual Results

```
1.7.0
--- Cross validation ---
Received metadata='Some metadata'
Received metadata=None
Received metadata='Some metadata'
Received metadata=None
```

title: Array API: Ridge with alpha as an array fails due to misconfigured checks
body:
### Describe the bug and give evidence about its user-facing impact

When trying to fit a ridge model with the array API on multi target objective, if one want to pass an array for alpha, the checks are not well configured I think, leading to an impossibility.
Here is a small reproducer.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
import os
os.environ["SCIPY_ARRAY_API"] = "1"

import torch

from sklearn import config_context
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge
from sklearn.datasets import make_regression

X, y = make_regression(1000, n_targets=10)
with config_context(array_api_dispatch=True):
    # will consider that alpha is scalar in `_ridge_regression`
    # and fail the check_scalar check l.703
    reg = Ridge(alpha=np.array([1.]*10))
    reg.fit(torch.tensor(X), torch.tensor(y))

     # Will fail on `_validate_params` from the base estimator class
    reg = Ridge(alpha=torch.tensor([1.]*10))
    reg.fit(torch.tensor(X), torch.tensor(y))
```

### Expected Results

Working with both cases :)

### Actual Results

raise errors:

title: Using dask distributed with a pipeline and column_transformer breaks metadata routing
body:
### Describe the bug and give evidence about its user-facing impact

when using a pipeline with a columns transformer, using the pipeline in a distributed environment seems to raise a strange error.

### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```python
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.compose import make_column_transformer
from sklearn.pipeline import make_pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, QuantileTransformer
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score

X, y = make_classification()

pipeline = make_pipeline(
    make_column_transformer(
        (StandardScaler(), [0]),
    ),
    # StandardScaler(),
    RandomForestClassifier(
        max_depth=7, n_estimators=300, random_state=42
    )
)
pipeline

cv_results = cross_val_score(pipeline, X, y, cv=5, n_jobs=1)
print("CV score:", np.mean(cv_results))

from distributed import Client
from joblib import parallel_config

client = Client()

with parallel_config("dask"):
    cv_scores = cross_val_score(pipeline, X, y, cv=5, n_jobs=-1)

print("CV score:", np.mean(cv_scores))
client.close()
```

title: Webview resource cache grows unboundedly: media-preview cache-busts with Date.now(), service worker never evicts (21+ GB observed)
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes
(media-preview and the webview service worker are built-in, so `--disable-extensions` does not affect the leak.)

- VS Code Version: 1.123.2 (commit 3c631b164c239e7aeaaae7c626b46c527b361af2, x64)
- OS Version: Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200 (also observed under WSL Remote – Ubuntu)

## Summary

`%APPDATA%\Code\WebStorage\<n>\CacheStorage` grew to **23.5 GB** on my machine.
I parsed the Chromium simple-cache entry keys to find out why. The root cause is a
combination of two pieces of built-in code:

1. **media-preview cache-busts with the current time, not the file version.**
   `extensions/media-preview/src/imagePreview/index.ts` (`getResourcePath`) appends
   `?version=${Date.now()}` on **every render**. Every time an image is opened or
   re-rendered, the URL is brand new — even if the file is unchanged.

2. **The webview service worker caches every response and never evicts.**
   `src/vs/workbench/contrib/webview/browser/pre/service-worker.js` does
   `cache.put(event.request, ...)` for every 200 response with an ETag, keyed by the
   full URL including the query string. The file contains no eviction, expiry, or
   quota logic. The `activate` handler doesn't even delete caches from previous
   `VERSION`s — my disk holds both `vscode-resource-cache-4` (2.09 GB) and
   `vscode-resource-cache-5` (21.38 GB).

Combined effect: **every view of an image permanently writes a full copy to disk**,
and each copy is dead on arrival — the `Date.now()` query string guarantees no future
request can ever hit it.

## Evidence (from parsing the cache entry keys)

My 21.38 GB cache origin contains 6,413 entries:

- They map to only **1,947 unique files** (1,013 files cached ≥ 2 times).
- The most-viewed image was cached **86 times within a single hour** (one evening,
  up to 9 copies in one minute) — per-render, not per-modification.
- In a sample of the 200 most-duplicated paths, **81% point to files that no longer
  exist** on disk.
- Zero video/audio entries: `<video>` uses Range requests (206), which the SW marks
  `no-store` and the Cache API cannot store anyway. The leak therefore only bites
  image-heavy workflows and grows slowly enough to evade detection — which is
  presumably why earlier reports (#131226, #166632 [145 GB], #152519 [500 GB]) died
  as "cannot reproduce" / stale.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Note the size of `%APPDATA%\Code\WebStorage` (`du -sh ~/.config/Code/WebStorage` on Linux).
2. Launch `code --disable-extensions` and open a large PNG (e.g. 20 MB) in the
   built-in image preview.
3. Close the tab and reopen the same file 10 times.
4. The folder grows by ~10 × the image size. None of the new entries can ever be
   read back (each was stored under a unique `?version=<timestamp>` URL), and they
   are never cleaned up.

## Expected

Either of:
- media-preview keys the cache-busting parameter on the file's mtime instead of
  `Date.now()`, so unchanged files reuse one cache entry; and/or
- the service worker evicts stale versions of the same base URL when caching a new
  one, applies an LRU/size cap to `vscode-resource-cache-*`, and deletes
  previous-`VERSION` caches on activate.

## Related

#131226 (closed as stale, "needs repro"), #166632 (145 GB, closed as duplicate),
#152519 (500 GB, closed as duplicate), #310384 (open, 30 GB via Remote-SSH — same
mechanism; this report adds the root-cause analysis).

title: No credits spent shown when a request is stopped in Auto mode
body:
No credits spent shown when a request is stopped.

When a chat request is halted by the user, the chat does not show which model it chose in Auto mode, nor how many credits it spent.

This is important, since I've seen models freeze or start thinking in a loop and i cannot see which model was the failing one, nor see how many credits it spent.


<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: v0.53.0
- VS Code Version: 1.125.0
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100 (Windows 11)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Chat
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): any
- Logs:

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Write a chat request, question, whatever using Auto for model selection.
2. Let it work for a little
3. Press the stop button to halt thinking and text/code generation
4. Look in the chat footnote for model and credits info, they are not there.

title: Noisy logs "Closing/Hiding terminals"
body:
Seeing many hundreds of log messages like this printed repeatedly. It doesn't make sense to me because I don't think that all of these sessions have terminals. How can a CCA agent have terminals?


title: [Bug] Model Picker Configuration Not Persisted to `chatLanguageModels.json` — Third-Party Providers Cannot Receive User-Configured Model Settings
body:
## Summary

When a user adjusts model parameters (e.g. **Reasoning Effort**, **Context Size**, **Service Tier**) via the **Chat Model Picker dropdown**, the values are correctly persisted for the current chat editor, but third-party `LanguageModelChatProvider` extensions receive the previously persisted value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default — not the user-selected value — in `modelConfiguration` at request time. The user-visible settings do not flow into the API surface used by non-Copilot providers.

---

## Regression Window

| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Last Known Good** | VS Code **1.124.2** (2026-06-10) |
| **First Known Bad** | VS Code **1.125.0** (2026-06-17) — also **1.125.1** |
| **Root Cause PR** | [#321011](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/321011) — *Fix model configurations syncing across editors and locations* |
| **Underlying Issue** | [#320393](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/320393) — *Context length selector changes on all open editors* |
| **Milestone** | [1.125.0](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/milestone/424) |
| **Files Changed** | `+725 / −58` across ~8 files |

### What the PR Did

PR [#321011](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/321011) introduced **per-editor scoped model configuration** to fix a bug where changing context size in one editor affected all other open editors. The key additions were:

- **`chatModelConfigurationStore.ts`** — Per-editor `IStorageService` bucket (resolves per `(location, sessionType)` key)
- **`chatModelConfigurationLogic.ts`** — Resolution logic: `resolveModelConfiguration()`, `computeStoredConfiguration()`, `filterConfigurationToSchema()`, `extractSchemaDefaults()`
- **`modelPickerActionItem.ts`** — `IModelConfigurationAccess` interface (read/write/actions contract)
- **Modifications to `chatInputPart.ts`** — Routes model picker reads/writes through `ChatModelConfigurationStore`
- **Modifications to `chatService.ts` / `chatServiceImpl.ts`** — Passes `userSelectedModelConfiguration` through to requests
- **Modifications to `languageModels.ts`** — Extracted `createModelConfigurationActions()` helper

### What Broke

Before 1.125.0, model picker wrote configuration **directly** via `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` → both the global `_modelConfigurations` Map and `chatLanguageModels.json`. After the change, the primary write targets the per-editor `IStorageService` bucket, and only *mirrors* to the global service as a secondary operation. The mirror write (`ChatModelConfigurationStore.setModelConfiguration()` → `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()`) has a **hidden dependency**: it requires the model's metadata to already exist in `_modelCache`. When `_modelCache` is not yet populated (e.g. provider registered asynchronously), `setModelConfiguration()` silently exits without writing anything.

| | **1.124.2 (Good)** | **1.125.0 (Bad)** |
|---|---|---|
| **Primary write** | `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` | `ChatModelConfigurationStore._applyLocalModelConfiguration()` → per-editor bucket |
| **Global mirror** | N/A (it was the primary) | `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` → ⚠️ depends on `_modelCache` hit |
| **Copilot reads** | `ChatRequest.modelConfiguration` ← ChatInputPart | `ChatRequest.modelConfiguration` ← `ChatModelConfigurationStore` ✅ |
| **3rd-party provider reads** | `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration` ← `_modelConfigurations` ✅ | Same path, but `_modelConfigurations` may be empty ⚠️ |

---

## Environment

- **VS Code Version**: ≥ 1.125.0 (Insiders / Stable)
- **OS**: All platforms
- **Affected APIs**: `vscode.lm.registerLanguageModelChatProvider` / `LanguageModelChatProvider`

---

## Root Cause Analysis

### The Silent Failure Path

| | **Channel ①: Per-Editor Bucket** | **Channel ②: Global `_modelConfigurations`** | **Channel ③: `chatLanguageModels.json`** |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Storage** | `IStorageService` KV store | In-memory `Map<string, IStringDictionary>` | Filesystem JSON |
| **Written by** | `ChatModelConfigurationStore._applyLocalModelConfiguration()` | `LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()` | `LanguageModelsConfigurationService` |
| **Write reliability** | ✅ Always succeeds | ⚠️ Depends on `_modelCache` hit | ⚠️ Depends on Channel ② |
| **Read by Copilot** | ✅ `userSelectedModelConfiguration` | ❌ Not used | ❌ Not used |
| **Read by 3rd-party LM providers** | ❌ Not accessible | ✅ `getModelConfiguration()` | ❌ Not read directly |

### The Silent Failure

The critical issue is in [`LanguageModelsService.setModelConfiguration()`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/languageModels.ts):

```typescript
async setModelConfiguration(modelId: string, values: IStringDictionary<unknown>): Promise<void> {
    const metadata = this._modelCache.get(modelId);
    if (!metadata) {
        return;  // ⚠️ SILENT RETURN — no write, no error, no warning!
    }
    // ...
    // 1. Update _modelConfigurations (in-memory)
    // 2. Write to chatLanguageModels.json
    // 3. Fire onDidChangeLanguageModels event
}
```

When `_modelCache` does not contain the model metadata (e.g., because the extension provider registered asynchronously and the cache was not yet populated, or was cleared during model re-resolution), the method **silently exits** without:
- Updating `_modelConfigurations` (the cache from which third-party providers read)  
- Writing to `chatLanguageModels.json`  
- Throwing or logging any error  

### Why Copilot Is Unaffected

Copilot participates via the `ChatParticipant` API. Its request path receives configuration through `ChatRequest.modelConfiguration`, which is sourced from `ChatInputPart.getModelConfiguration()` → `ChatModelConfigurationStore.getModelConfiguration()` — the **per-editor bucket (Channel ①)**:

```typescript
// chatServiceImpl.ts
modelConfiguration: options?.userSelectedModelConfiguration
    ?? (options?.userSelectedModelId
        ? this.languageModelsService.getModelConfiguration(options.userSelectedModelId)
        : undefined),
```

The primary value comes from `userSelectedModelConfiguration` (Channel ①), which is always available. The fallback to `getModelConfiguration()` (Channel ②) is only used when that primary source is absent.

### Why Third-Party Providers Are Affected

Third-party `LanguageModelChatProvider` implementations receive configuration through `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration`, which is populated from `LanguageModelsService.getModelConfiguration()` — the **global `_modelConfigurations` cache (Channel ②)**:

```typescript
// languageModelsService.sendChatRequest()
async sendChatRequest(modelId, from, messages, options, token) {
    const configuration = this.getModelConfiguration(modelId);
    const mergedOptions = configuration
        ? { ...options, configuration: { ...configuration, ...options.configuration } }
        : options;
    return provider.sendChatRequest(modelId, messages, from, mergedOptions, token);
}
```

If Channel ② was never populated with the user's latest picker selection (due to the silent failure above), `modelConfiguration` arrives as the previously persisted value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default.

---

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install a third-party extension that registers a `LanguageModelChatProvider` with a `configurationSchema` (e.g. one providing `reasoningEffort` and `contextSize` properties).
2. Open the Chat view and select the third-party model from the model picker.
3. In the model picker dropdown, change **Reasoning Effort** or **Context Size** to a non-default value (e.g., `max`).
4. Send a chat request using this model.
5. Observe in the extension's handler that `options.modelConfiguration` contains the previously persisted value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default — never the value just selected in the picker.

### Expected

- Third-party providers should receive the user-selected configuration values from the model picker in `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration`.

### Actual

- `options.modelConfiguration` at request time contains the value from `chatLanguageModels.json` or the schema default, not the value the user just selected in the model picker.
- The model picker UI shows the correct selected value (because it reads from the per-editor bucket), but the actual request uses the persisted/default value visible to third-party providers.

---

## Proposed Fix

### Short-term

At minimum, `setModelConfiguration()` should **log a warning** when `_modelCache` is missing a model, so the silent failure is visible. Additionally, consider retrying the write when the model becomes available:

```typescript
async setModelConfiguration(modelId: string, values: IStringDictionary<unknown>): Promise<void> {
    const metadata = this._modelCache.get(modelId);
    if (!metadata) {
        this._logService.warn(`[LM] Cannot persist configuration for '${modelId}': model not yet resolved in cache`);
        // Optionally: queue the write for when the model resolves
        return;
    }
    // ...
}
```

### Long-term

1. **Unify the two configuration channels** so there is a single source of truth for per-model configuration, accessible equally to Copilot and third-party providers.
2. **Expose per-editor model configuration** to extensions through a stable API, so `LanguageModelChatProvider` implementations can access the user's current selection without relying on the global cache.
3. **Make `chatLanguageModels.json` or a unified service the canonical persistence layer** for all model configuration, ensuring that changes from the model picker are always written to the file/service and are consistently read by all consumers, including third-party providers.

---

## Related Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/input/chatModelConfigurationStore.ts` | Per-editor scoped storage (Channel ①) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/widget/input/chatModelConfigurationLogic.ts` | Resolution, filtering, schema extraction |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/languageModels.ts` | `setModelConfiguration()` / `getModelConfiguration()` / `sendChatRequest()` (Channel ②) |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/languageModelsConfigurationService.ts` | `chatLanguageModels.json` read/write (Channel ③) |
| `src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostLanguageModels.ts` | Extension host: `$startChatRequest` — bridges `modelConfiguration` to provider |
| `src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadLanguageModels.ts` | Main thread: registers provider & proxies `sendChatRequest` |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatServiceImpl.ts` | Chat service: merges `userSelectedModelConfiguration` → `modelConfiguration` |

---

## Impact

This bug affects **all third-party `LanguageModelChatProvider` extensions** that declare a `configurationSchema` and rely on `ProvideLanguageModelChatResponseOptions.modelConfiguration` to receive user-configured model parameters (reasoning effort, context size, service tier, etc.).

title: Copilot Chat auto-switches to 1M context after VS Code restart, ignoring selected model setting
body:
* Copilot Chat Extension Version: latest
* VS Code Version: latest
* OS Version: latest
* Feature: Copilot Chat / existing chat session after VS Code restart
* Selected model: GPT 5.5 High 272k

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set Copilot Chat to use GPT 5.5 High with 272k context.
2. Start or continue a Copilot Chat session.
3. Restart VS Code.
4. Continue the existing Copilot Chat session.
5. Check the model/context shown in the Copilot UI.

## Expected Behavior

Copilot should respect the currently selected model/context setting after VS Code restart.

If I selected GPT 5.5 High 272k, continuing an existing session should keep using GPT 5.5 High 272k, unless I explicitly choose another model/context.

## Actual Behavior

After restarting VS Code and continuing an existing session, Copilot appears to ignore the selected setting and silently switches to the 1M context option.

This is especially serious because 1M is much more expensive. The switch is not explicitly confirmed by the user, but the user is still charged for the more expensive option.

## Impact

This caused unexpected billing charges for me. In one case, I paid around $10 because Copilot silently switched to the 1M option.

This makes Copilot unsafe to use with paid models/context tiers, because the UI setting is not reliably respected across VS Code restarts.

## Request

Please investigate why continuing an existing session after VS Code restart can override the user-selected model/context setting.

At minimum, Copilot should never silently switch to a more expensive model/context tier without explicit user confirmation.
